chief_complaint: A 1-month-old female infant presented with a month-long history of dry cough, along with two episodes of fever and increased work of breathing.
history: The infant had a history of severe acute malnutrition. She was admitted to the hospital and required oxygen therapy. She had two episodes of fever and increased work of breathing.
exam: On examination, she had mild tachypnoea, chest retractions, and reduced air entry in the left lower lung zone with occasional wheezing. Her oxygen saturation was normal on room air.
labs: Chest X-ray showed left lung hyperinflation with mediastinal shift to the right. A plain CT chest done outside showed left lung hyperlucency. Bronchoscopy revealed near-complete blockage of the left main bronchus. Contrast chest CT identified a hypodense lesion in the subcarinal area, compressing the oesophagus and left main bronchus and abutting the aorta.
